key_insight: PhononScore is a fast, phonon-aware scoring function that ranks crystal candidates by dynamical stability without explicit phonon calculations.
engineering_relevance: It gives materials-discovery pipelines a second-level, stability-aware filter for reranking, active learning, and reinforcement learning in high-throughput inverse design.
method: Trains a unified stability scorer on a multi-fidelity phonon dataset of 157,463 crystal structures.
Predicts dynamical stability directly from crystal structures instead of running expensive phonon computations.
Provides a feedback signal for crystal generation model reranking and can be finetuned to DFT-quality phonon data.
evidence_signal: On PhononBench, it raised average stability from 30.7% to 83.7% across nine generators, and Top-10 stability reached 97.5%; the DFT-finetuned version reached 93.0% Top-100 stability on a high-fidelity benchmark.
watch_item: Performance depends on benchmark coverage and training fidelity, so extrapolation to out-of-distribution chemistries or structures may still require explicit phonon validation.
paper_title: PhononScore: a phonon-aware scoring function for dynamical stability
